what_it_does: This file is a captured noticeboard item that records and hydrates Tim's 2026-06-17 transmission describing the first part of the Substrate: an addressed, typed, queryable repository architecture with registries, a repo-census workflow, typed edges, wayfinding guides, and SQL storage.
observations: YAML frontmatter includes id item-d65629c4, a board:// address, type idea, source claude_code, state captured, author DNA, and a link attached_to SUBSTRATE-ARCHITECTURE.md.
The title frames the content as Tim's transmission, verbatim plus hydration.
The body summarizes an addressed, typed graph model where addresses are relative paths from root.

It mentions two seeded registries: address-registry.json with 605 addresses and type-registry.json with 22 types, including special names like .gitignore, README.md, build.sh, manifest.json, and serve.sh.
A repo-census workflow is described: territory-allocated Sonnet agents emit one objective per-file record covering structure, format, content counts, and typed directional edges.
Folder/file duality is placed under Directory along the prime contains axis.
Type entries carry declarations for parsers, format, fields, valid edges, and interpretations.
The registry itself has rules: valid types, ignore patterns, and prompt resolution.
Typed edges below the prime axis are directional and equal-opposite, such as import/export.
Unresolved edge targets become GHOST NODES, a special type that can exist in many registries.
Attachments are treated as a universal extension for observations and notes.
CLAUDE.md at root and per-folder carries the substrate guide so agents can wayfind.
The architecture is said to build on lineage laws: derive-never-place, registry-is-truth, equal-opposite, and the two halves.
Persistent storage is planned for SQL/Postgres.
standouts: Claims lineage-derived laws such as derive-never-place, registry-is-truth, equal-opposite, and the two halves.
Introduces GHOST NODES as a special type for unresolved edge targets.
Proposes a repo-census of per-file Sonnet agents producing objective records and typed attachments.
Treats directory/file duality as a typed prime axis with equal-opposite contains edges.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, history, timestamps, and linked attachments.
Uses a board:// URI scheme for the item address.
ISO 8601 timestamps with explicit timezone offset.
Markdown body with a short title and a dense, hyphen-separated summary.
References counterpart design docs via relative path links.
concerns: Contains subjective valuation (EXTREMELY HIGH VALUE) in a record meant to be neutral.
Channel and thread fields are empty, leaving context placement unspecified.
Dense jargon and lineage-specific terms are not defined within the file.
Numeric claims (605 addresses, 22 types) are asserted without source evidence in the file.
notes: A dense architectural seed entry that maps Tim's substrate vision into a metadata-backed noticeboard item with registries, census, and typed edges.
questions: What is the exact JSON schema for address-registry.json and type-registry.json?
How are equal-opposite edges validated when one side is missing?
What distinguishes a GHOST NODE from an ordinary unresolved file entry?
What are the two halves referenced in the lineage laws?
How does the repo-census agent allocate territory and resolve conflicts?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to capture and hydrate Tim's transmission about the first part of the Substrate; the file does exactly that, adding a structured noticeboard wrapper and a compressed summary of concepts and artifacts.
apparent_themes: Registry as single source of truth
intention_signals: To establish a machine-readable foundational layer for a codebase where every file has a stable address, type, and typed edges, supported by automated agents, registries, and wayfinding guides, so the project becomes self-describing and queryable.
novelty: It unifies address/type registries, a per-file agent census, GHOST NODES for unresolved targets, equal-opposite typed edges, and CLAUDE.md wayfinding into one substrate architecture, treating directory/file duality as a first-class typed axis.
contribution: It preserves an early, high-signal architectural transmission in a structured, addressable noticeboard item, links it to a design document, and enumerates the substrate's core concepts so later queries can trace the origin and intent of the registry/census/edge model.
summary_for_embedding: A captured noticeboard idea item that records Tim's 2026-06-17 transmission describing the first part of the Substrate, an addressed, typed, queryable repository with address and type registries, a repo-census workflow, equal-opposite typed edges, GHOST NODES, CLAUDE.md wayfinding, and SQL/Postgres storage.
intention_for_embedding: To document and hydrate the foundational substrate architecture so it can be referenced, queried, and implemented by agent-driven census and registry workflows.
